extra.reasoning: The mutual exclusion property requires that no input makes both rec_drug_a and rec_drug_b true. The only rule setting rec_drug_a is r3 (priority 50: IF anticoagulated THEN rec_drug_a := true), and the only rule setting rec_drug_b is r4 (priority 50: IF anticoagulated THEN rec_drug_b := true). Both have the identical guard condition: anticoagulated. Therefore any input with anticoagulated = true fires both r3 and r4, setting rec_drug_a = true and rec_drug_b = true simultaneously. No other rule can unset either output (defaults apply only to outputs left unset, and these are set by r3/r4). With anticoagulated=true, prior_gi_bleed=false, pregnancy=false: r3 sets rec_drug_a=true, r4 sets rec_drug_b=true, and rec_drug_c defaults to false. This makes (rec_drug_a AND rec_drug_b) jointly true, directly violating the property.
